text:
Jan × Ken × Rock (ジャン×ケン×グー, Jan × Ken × Gū) is the 85 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and the 7 th episode of the Greed Island final OVA series. It was released on DVD on April 28 th , 2004.

Overview

Gon uses his new abilities in the dodgeball game. Killua's hands are heavily injured. Only Razor remains on his team, facing Gon, Killua, and Hisoka.

Synopsis

Razor launches his attack at Gon, who successfully blocks the dodgeball. However, the impact sends Gon flying out of the arena, crashing through a wall as the ball sails over the ceiling.

Razor explains that he had calculated the force required for Gon to block the attack. Since Gon successfully used his Nen to block the ball, Razor remarks that Gon should be fine. Biscuit and Killua rush to check on Gon, but he reassures them that he's okay. He explains that he protected his head and hands with Nen, but lost his footing, leaving his legs unguarded and vulnerable to Razor's attack. Regardless, Gon is assured that he will catch Razor's next attack. Much to his surprise, Tsezguerra hinted that he couldn't believe Gon survived the attack.

As the match is about to resume, Gon is automatically ruled out due to where the ball from the last play landed. However, despite Biscuit's objections, he stubbornly uses "Back" to re-enter the match later. Noticing Gon's determination, which Killua also points out, Biscuit shifts her approach and advises Gon on how to use "Back" more strategically in the future. Gon takes her advice and agrees. 

As the match resumes, Gon and Killua's team gains possession of the ball. To buy Gon some time to recover, Killua and Goreinu pass the ball back and forth while Gon receives assistance from Tsezguerra's team off the court.

Goreinu recalls a previous play where Razor hit him hard, fueling his determination to return the favor. Charging forward to launch his attack, he targets Black Goreinu. Just as the ball is about to hit, he swaps Black Goreinu's position with Razor's, causing Razor to take the hit directly to the face. Goreinu boastfully points this out, and Razor acknowledges Goreinu's ability. However, before the ball touches the ground, one of Razor's teammates swiftly catches it and immediately hurls it at Goreinu. Caught off-guard, Goreinu takes the hit to the face and is sent flying off the court, unconscious. Razor praises Goreinu's ability while noting his Nen ability. Black and White Goreinu disappears from the field. Razor suspects that this outcome is linked to Goreinu's unconscious state.

Biscuit immediately questions Razor about his last play, but Razor insists that he did nothing wrong and is still playing by the rules. He explains every detail, particularly highlighting the differences between Goreinu's attack and his own. Meanwhile, Goreinu is carried off the court, still unconscious, and it was noted by Razor that once he wakes up, he can get back to the field, but also points out that it's highly unlikely since he won't get up soon. The referee pointed to the players and said that the match would end once all infielders from the team reached zero.

Rodriot analyzes their team situation as despite the match being three vs. three, they're still at a disadvantage since half of their players have retired, and Gon is injured while still out. He also stated Razor's team advantage. Razor and his team face off against Killua, Biscuit, and Hisoka on the field. As Razor prepares to attack, he taunts his opponents, asking, "Who's next?" When he strikes, his attack targets Killua, who feels uncertain about blocking it. At the last moment, Killua decides to dodge the incoming ball. To his surprise, the ball swerves 90 degrees toward Biscuit and Hisoka.

Both Biscuit and Hisoka quickly notice the ball and successfully evade it. However, as it passes by them, one of Razor's teammates teleports to catch the ball and immediately strikes Hisoka. In response, Hisoka activates his Bungee Gum, successfully catching the ball despite being pushed back by the force. After the play, Killua wonders what Razor is plotting. Analyzing the previous sequence, he realizes that if he had dodged in the wrong direction, he would have been hit by the ball. Unfortunately, during the last exchange, Biscuit was struck by the ball, tearing part of her skirt. Acknowledging the rules that consider clothing part of one's body, she calmly accepts the loss.

Despite their challenging situation, Rodriot, Kess, and Barry encourage Hisoka and Killua. Suddenly, Gon appears, having finally recovered, and approaches the field with laser-like focus. As he enters, Biscuit, who is leaving the field, checks on him and asks if he's okay. Gon responds positively with a simple "yeah". Knowing Gon well, Biscuit follows up with a trick question: "What is one plus one?" Gon responds with "yeah" once more. In response, Biscuit gently taps Gon and encourages him to defeat Razor.

Gon joins Killua and Hisoka on the field. After catching the ball, Hisoka explains to the duo that its power diminishes when the outfielders relay the ball. He adds that he injured two fingers without using Nen and emphasizes that they can avoid heavy damage if they watch out for Razor's throws. However, Gon counters that if they play it safe, it won't feel like a victory. Killua objects to Gon's perspective, prompting Gon to remind him of a previous situation where Razor targeted him, leading to Biscuit's elimination from the field. Gon expresses his desire to win and completely defeat their opponents. Sensing the tension, Hisoka intervenes, convinced that Gon has a plan, to which Gon confirms.

As Hisoka passes the ball to Gon, he instructs Killua to stand in a specific position on the field. Killua takes his place, firmly holding the ball as Gon requested. Once they are positioned, Gon charges his Nen, preparing to hit the ball and catch Razor's and everyone else's attention. Gon channels his Nen ability and strikes the ball with his Rock Nen, hitting one of Razor's teammates. Despite catching the ball, the force of Gon's attack knocks him out of the field due to the impact.

Gon's team possesses the ball, and his teammates off the field celebrate the last play and Gon's efforts. However, Gon isn't satisfied; he knows he needs more to defeat Razor. Biscuit, who is out of the game, throws the ball to Killua. Killua steps back from his stance, firmly holding the ball, and encourages Gon to deliver a powerful strike to Razor, enough to take him down. Gon acknowledges Killua's motivation and agrees.

On the other side, Razor is poised to receive Gon's strike, while Gon tells himself that he needs to harness more power to defeat Razor. Razor notices Gon with his eyes closed, releasing an immense amount of aura, which others also begin to notice. Outside the court, Biscuit confronts Razor and taunts him, claiming he chose the wrong sport. She reminds him that Gon can take all the time he needs to maximize his strength in this game. 

Continuing her taunts, she asks Razor if he will dodge Gon's attack, to which Razor confidently hints that he will take the hit and not dodge. Gon charges his attack while Killua holds the ball firmly. Gon strikes the ball with immense power, hurting it toward Razor. Razor, fully prepared, waits for the ball and successfully counters Gon's attack. He cushions the impact by pressing his arms against his body without flinching, perfectly timing his parry. The ball is sent soaring into the air, prompting both Hisoka and Biscuit to acknowledge Razor's tactical skill and strength. Gon is smiling and amazed at Razor. Razor explains that catching and dodging aren't the only options for this game, while Gon is still amazed at what Razor shown. Off the field, Tsezguerra and his team analyze Razor's tactics and sum up that how Razor receives the ball has killed its force.

As the ball hung in the air, out of nowhere, Hisoka took the ball in the air using his bungee gum, putting Razor off guard and teasing him that he needed to hold the ball firmly. Razor, seemingly baffled, was automatically out, but soon used one last ‘back' to re-enter the match. Rodriot and the others seemingly thought that the situation was now to their advantage; however, Tsezguerra pointed out that they were wrong since their team was the one who wounded, adding that Hisoka wouldn't be able to throw anymore or even can't be able to catch the ball with his right hand, while Gon using a lot of power will soon be fatigued and stated that he could not make another attack like the last one then Kess responded that only the one who is in good condition was Killua. Still, Tsezguerra disagrees and concludes that Killua is the most hurt of the three.

Gon and Killua talk to each other; Killua is determined to help Gon defeat Razor and tell himself he can hold the ball for him again. Tsezguerra explains that Killua had it worst because he was holding the ball without covering his hands with nen when Gon is hitting the ball with an immense aura, he adds that if Killua guards his arms with too much aura it will hinder's Gon's punch and won't hold as much power. Knowing this, Tsezguerra blames himself and says he should have been there instead of Killua as he still lacks the proper technique. Biscuits asks Hisoka to pass him the ball and immediately throws the ball toward one of Razor's teammates, who gets eliminated.

With Razor now the sole remaining member of his team, the stage is set for the final battle. Tsezguerra calls for a timeout and approaches the field to discuss strategy. Killua urges Tsezguerra to rest and heal, but Tsezguerra insists that his condition is far better than Killua's. Seeking clarification on the rules, Tsezguerra inquires whether an infielder can move to the outfield independently. The referee confirms that while this is permissible, an infielder cannot return to the infield once they have moved to the outfield. Killua questions the purpose of Tsezguerra's inquiry, prompting Tsezguerra to promise an explanation later and request that they follow him.

Tsezguerra then explains the situation, highlighting Killua's hand injuries. He asserts that he should take over the responsibility of holding the ball to prevent further damage to Killua's hands. However, Killua adamantly refuses the suggestion, and Gon supports his friend's decision. Despite this, Tsezguerra appears in considerable pain, flinching as he speaks. Killua urges him not to push himself, given his own compromised state. Tsezguerra insists on examining Killua's hands, but Killua declines. With determination, Biscuit grabs Killua's hand from his pocket and reveals the extent of his injuries. Stubbornly, Killua maintains that he can manage one more shot and turns to Gon, prompting everyone to await his response. Meanwhile, Razor observes from a distance, contemplating how Gon will navigate this pivotal moment.
